paper_title: Foraging Behavior and Pollen Analysis of Stingless Bees

methods.description: This method involves the collection of pollen foragers from stingless bee colonies to study their foraging behavior across different seasons.

steps.description: Sampling was carried out in the months of June, August, October, and November 2015 and May 2016, covering both dry and rainy seasons.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field sampling of pollen foragers during specified months

steps.description: We selected 3 meliponaries, spaces for the breeding of stingless bees for honey production, separated 500 m from each other, for evaluating the foraging behavior of both species.
steps.searchable_summary: select meliponaries for bee foraging behavior study

steps.description: Pollen foragers returning to the hive were captured at the nest entrances using a hand-net between 8:30 and 14:00 h.
steps.searchable_summary: capture pollen foragers using hand-net at specified times

steps.description: From each colony, samples were collected from two pollen sacs of foragers bees, and each sample was individually placed in a 2-ml Eppendorf tube, duly labeled, and brought back to the laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and label pollen sac samples from foragers

methods.description: This method describes the process of identifying and characterizing pollen from the surrounding habitat to create a reference collection.

steps.description: Floral resources identification in the surrounding habitat was done through a radial track of 2 km around the colonies.
steps.searchable_summary: identify floral resources within 2 km radius of colonies

steps.description: For each plant species with floral resources, blossoms and flowers were taken for pollen characterization.
steps.searchable_summary: collect blossoms and flowers for pollen characterization

steps.description: We used these samples to create a pollen reference collection for our study.
steps.searchable_summary: create pollen reference collection from samples

methods.description: This method involves the preparation and analysis of pollen samples in the laboratory to determine pollen morphotypes and their abundance.

steps.description: In the laboratory, corbicular pollen samples from each individual were diluted in alcohol and mounted on three microscope slides.
steps.searchable_summary: dilute pollen samples in alcohol and mount on slides

steps.description: Once the alcohol evaporated, we set the pollen using 2 μl of glycerogelatin solution; the center of each slide was stained with fuchsin.
steps.searchable_summary: set pollen with glycerogelatin and stain with fuchsin

steps.description: The classification of pollen morphotypes was done using a Zeiss-Axiostar Plus optical microscope with a 40× objective lens.
steps.searchable_summary: classify pollen morphotypes using optical microscope

steps.description: The number of grains of each morphotype was recorded for each slide, and relative abundance of each representative plant taxon found in the pollen sample was quantified for the three slides.
steps.searchable_summary: record and quantify pollen grain abundance

methods.description: This method involves the taxonomic identification of pollen morphotypes and statistical analysis of pollen types collected by bees.

steps.description: Taxonomic identification of pollen morphotypes found on stingless bees to the level of family, genus, or species was done by comparing them to the hand-collected pollen of plants in the area and, in some cases, with information about the characteristics of the family.
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen morphotypes taxonomically by comparison

steps.description: Additionally, we obtained the mean and SD of pollen morphotypes visited by individual bees.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean and SD of pollen morphotypes

steps.description: We calculated the number of pollen types collected by an individual bee by summing the pollen types found on the three slides examined per bee.
steps.searchable_summary: sum pollen types per bee from slides
